input.tenant_message: Two of the wooden stairs on the interior staircase have cracked and one of them feels like it might give way when I step on it — it sags in the middle and makes a loud cracking noise. The handrail is also pulling away from the wall on one side. My elderly mother visits regularly and I'm very concerned about this.

ai_output.recommended_action: Treat this as a fall and injury hazard requiring same-day repair or restriction. If the staircase is the only access between floors, arrange temporary safe use protocols and dispatch a carpenter immediately to replace the damaged treads and re-secure the handrail bracket. Inspect stair stringers for underlying rot or failure.
